scope: ACL supplement systematic visual scan for obvious occlusion, unsafe scaling, and incomplete figure display.

finding.issue: The Material salience row used cover-style crop fitting for wide supplemental material strips, making clearcoat and procedural material panels appear as narrow vertical slivers in the rendered PDF.

explicit_rechecks.result: The bottom claim-boundary gate remains fully visible in the final PDF crop.
The material row now preserves the wide supplemental material strips using contain/letterbox placement instead of cover-cropping them into slivers.

changes: Changed the two MATERIAL_SUPPLEMENTAL crops in _compose_material_bridge from _crop_fit to _crop_contain.
Added a regression assertion to tests/test_paper_layout.py for contain placement in the theory bridge material row.
Regenerated fig_supplement_theory_evidence_bridge.png and rebuilt the ACL supplement PDF.

verification.result: passed after failing on the old _crop_fit placement

verification.command: rg -n "Overfull|Float too large|too large|LaTeX Warning: Float|LaTeX Warning: Reference|Warning:.*Label|undefined|Rerun to get|Label\\(s\\) may have changed|Package lineno Warning" paper/venues/acl27/build/supplement.log || true

residual_risk: Dense supplement index pages remain compact by design. This pass did not find another blocking crop, page-edge overflow, caption collision, or incomplete-display defect in the reviewed high-risk pages.
